user_story_groups.story: When a future Codexter system is runtime-like, I need a reusable template that tells me which domain, state, config, failure, observability, and conformance sections belong in the spec.
When reading the PRD, I should not be forced through service-runtime implementation details that belong in specs.
When implementing a ticket sliced from a complex spec, I need the ticket to carry concrete proof rows, not the whole system spec copied into the ticket body.
When reviewing future specs, I need a conformance matrix to catch missing config, state, failure, and test coverage before implementation starts.

summary: The spec authoring contract provides a useful depth decision and conformance matrix template without forcing Symphony-style ceremony onto PRDs or small tickets. Skill pointers are minimal and route future agents to the canonical doc instead of duplicating long instructions.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The guide names the layer split and spec depth levels clearly enough for future agents to decide when heavyweight sections are warranted.
rubric_sections.next_action: Use the contract for complex system specs and keep simple tickets light.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The skill updates are small pointers rather than duplicated policy blocks.
rubric_sections.next_action: Avoid expanding the skill pointers unless a repeated miss proves they are too thin.

rubric_sections.name: user-intent-satisfaction
findings: The result adopts the useful Symphony spec discipline while explicitly preserving PRD and ticket lightness.
rubric_sections.next_action: Apply the template to later runtime/service specs, not to every doc.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Doc parity, harness invariants, registry validators, and ticket metadata are enough proof for a docs/governance ticket.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep the validation commands in ticket Evidence.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The contract is linked from the specs index, skill pointers, techniques inventory, feature registry, and source registry.

rubric_sections.name: debloatability
findings: The guide centralizes conformance guidance in one spec rather than copying long templates into PRD, tickets, or multiple skills.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep future additions in this contract unless a local module needs a narrow pointer.

commands.command: feature registry validation snippet from docs/features/README.md

commands.result: structural doc parity OK (6 files checked, 29 rules)
harness invariants OK (5 files checked, 15 agents, 13 rules)

next_action: Close TASK-0116 and commit the spec-authoring contract slice.
